meta.name: Classic Lasagna (Meat Ragù + Béchamel)
meta.subtitle: Layered pasta bake with slow-simmered tomato-meat sauce and creamy béchamel

source.name: ssa-core
notes: Canonical baseline Italian baked pasta for SSA catalogs.
Designed for deterministic planning: make-ahead friendly, batch-friendly, freezer-friendly.
Uses béchamel (classic in many regional styles). Ricotta swap provided as variation.

yield.notes: One 9x13-inch (23x33 cm) pan. Serving size varies by sides.

notes: Best as a weekend cook or batch cook: sauce + béchamel can be made ahead.
Freezes well baked or unbaked; thaw overnight before baking when possible.
Balance tomato acidity with slow simmer; optional pinch of sugar if needed.
Nutmeg is classic in béchamel; keep subtle.

ingredients.label: Ground pork (or Italian sausage, casings removed)
ingredients.notes: About 0.75 lb. If using sausage, reduce added salt.

ingredients.label: Tomato passata (optional)
ingredients.notes: Use for a smoother ragù. If using, reduce crushed tomatoes by ~700 g.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Start with ~2 tsp and adjust. If sausage is salty, use less.

ingredients.label: Whole milk
ingredients.notes: About 4 cups. 2% works; whole is silkier.

ingredients.label: Nutmeg, freshly grated (optional)
ingredients.notes: A few microplane passes; keep subtle.

ingredients.label: Lasagna noodles
notes: About 12–15 noodles depending on pan and brand.
No-boil noodles are fine; see variations.

ingredients.label: Egg (optional, for richer structure in ricotta variation)
ingredients.notes: Only used if you choose ricotta variation.

ingredients.label: Ricotta cheese (optional, for ricotta-style lasagna variation)

steps.title: Prep aromatics and preheat
instructions: Dice onion, celery, and carrot finely (soffritto). Mince garlic.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Lightly oil a 9x13-inch baking dish.

steps.title: Sweat soffritto
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot or deep skillet over medium heat.
Add onion, celery, and carrot with a pinch of salt. Cook 6–8 minutes until softened and sweet.
Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Brown the meat
instructions: Add ground beef and pork. Cook, breaking it up, until browned and most liquid has evaporated.
Season with black pepper (go easy on salt if using sausage).
checks: Fond (brown bits) forming lightly on the pot is good.

steps.title: Deglaze, add tomato, and simmer ragù
instructions: Stir in tomato paste and cook 1–2 minutes to bloom.
Add wine (or broth). Scrape up browned bits and simmer 1–2 minutes.
Add crushed tomatoes, oregano, bay leaves, and salt (start with half; adjust later).
Bring to a gentle simmer and cook 25–35 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened.
checks: Sauce tastes less raw and has body.
Salt adjusted after simmer to avoid over-salting.

steps.title: Make béchamel (white sauce)
instructions: In a saucepan over medium heat, melt butter.
Whisk in flour to form a smooth roux. Cook 1–2 minutes (do not brown).
Gradually whisk in milk, a little at a time, until smooth.
Simmer gently 6–10 minutes, whisking often, until thick enough to coat a spoon.
Season with salt, black pepper, and nutmeg (optional). Stir in about 40 g of Parmesan (save the rest for layering).
checks: Sauce is smooth and coats spoon.

steps.title: Prepare noodles
instructions: If using regular lasagna noodles: cook in salted water until just pliable (a bit under al dente), then drain and lay flat to prevent sticking.
If using no-boil noodles: ensure your ragù is slightly looser (add 120–240 ml water/broth if needed) so noodles hydrate during baking.
steps.notes: Exact noodle handling depends on brand. Avoid overcooking noodles; they continue baking in the oven.

steps.title: Assemble lasagna
instructions: Spread a thin layer of ragù on the bottom of the baking dish.
Add a layer of noodles.
Add a layer of ragù, then drizzle/spread a layer of béchamel, then sprinkle mozzarella and a little Parmesan.
Repeat layers (noodles → ragù → béchamel → cheeses) until you reach the top (typically 3–4 noodle layers).
Finish with béchamel + mozzarella + Parmesan on top.
checks: Top has enough béchamel/cheese to prevent drying.
Layers are even; noodles covered with sauce (especially for no-boil).

steps.title: Bake
instructions: Cover with foil (tent slightly if cheese might stick).
Bake 35 minutes covered, then uncover and bake 15–20 minutes until bubbling and browned on top.
If you want deeper browning, broil 1–3 minutes at the end (watch closely).

steps.title: Rest, slice, and serve
instructions: Rest 20–30 minutes before slicing (crucial for clean layers).
Slice into squares and serve.
checks: Slices hold together better after resting.

notes: Cook ground meat thoroughly; sauce simmer plus bake should bring to safe temperature.
If using an internal thermometer, target at least 165°F (74°C) in the center.
Cool leftovers promptly; refrigerate within 2 hours.
Cool, cover, and refrigerate up to 4 days.
Reheat covered in oven at 350°F (175°C) until hot, or microwave portions with a splash of water.
Freeze portions or whole pan (well-wrapped) up to 3 months.
Thaw overnight in fridge for best texture; reheat covered until hot.
Ragù can be made 1–3 days ahead; béchamel best same-day (or 1 day ahead with gentle reheat).
Assemble unbaked lasagna up to 24 hours ahead; bake from cold with +10–15 minutes covered.
For a lighter lasagna, reduce béchamel and increase ragù slightly.
For a deeper flavor, simmer ragù longer (45–90 minutes) if time allows; add a splash of water if it gets too thick.
Mozzarella: low-moisture shred melts best; fresh mozzarella can weep (pat dry).
Replace béchamel with ricotta mixture for a common Italian-American style.
Mix ricotta + egg + parsley + a pinch of salt/pepper; layer dollops/spread thinly.
Ensure ragù is looser than usual so noodles hydrate.
Add 120–240 ml water/broth to the ragù if it is thick.
Replace meat with sautéed mushrooms (cremini/portobello) and spinach.
Add extra umami with a little soy sauce or miso (if allowed) or extra Parmesan.

addIngredients.label: Spinach
addIngredients.notes: Fresh or frozen (thawed and squeezed dry).

freezerNotes: Freeze in single portions for fast reheat.
